LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DIMITRIOU & ASSOCIATES, PC'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHOWS THAT THE DEFAULT TAKEN AGAINST IT SHOULD BE VACATED PER THE MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PROVISIONS OF CCP 437(B). THE COMBINATION OF PLAINTIFF'S EXTREME RUSH TO OBTAIN ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFENDANT'S EFFORT TO FILE ITS ANSWER A DAY AFTER IT WAS DUE PRESENT AN OVERWHELMING CASE WHERE THE DEFAULT SHOULD BE SET ASIDE. THE STRONG CALIFORNIA POLICY FAVORING DECISION OF CASES ON THEIR MERITS RATHER THAN TECHNICAL DEFAULTS CONFIRMS THAT THE MOTION SHOULD BE GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THE MOTION SEEKS TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT JUDGMENT, NO DEFAULT JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Prove Up Hearing For Entry Of Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2016 Continued To Feb-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgement
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Cebrian of Jones & Devoy LLP telephone number 415-615-0879 for Plaintiff Jones & Devoy LLP.
Andrew Dimitriou of Dimitriou & Associates telephone number 415-434-1144 for Defendant Dimitriou & Associates.

The matter was argued. Upon conclsuion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendant Dimitriou & Associates, PC's motion to vacate default is granted. Defendant shows that the default taken against it should be vacated per the mandatory and discretionary relief provisions of CCP 437(b). The combination of plaintiff's extreme rush to obtain entry of default and defendant's effort to file its answer a day after it was due present an overwhelming case where the default should be set aside. The strong California policy favoring decision of cases on their merits rather than technical defaults confirms that the motion should be granted. Although the motion seeks to set aside a default judgment, no default judgment has been entered. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
